column6: Description

HK416 Model designed to build on top of an already successful platform.
Customizable to various degrees.
Russian-made Modular Rifle made to replace the AK-74M. 5.45 Calibur Rounds with Solid Stats all around.

Company Produced Arma-Lite Rifles, in use by Island Security. 
Does not excel anywhere, but extremely customizable.
Bullpup Design. It's inherent design gives the user better stability.

Germany's Old Service Rifle. Most popular with the Bundeswehr. 
While not as customizable as an AR, It's stats are excellent.

AK Systems rifle utilizing a build similar to that of the 74. 
Uses 7.62 rounds to put holes in people.
Russian Military Surplus Rifle. Relatively Cheap to obtain and easy to use.
New Expiramental Assault Rifle looking to be utilized by British Special Forces. Modular, and uses 5.56 rounds

AR Platform designed with a Shorter Barrel. 
Suitable for CQB Enviroments with a Lighter Weight Focus

New Prototype Rifle going through field tests. 
Uses .300 BLK rounds, with an internally surpressed barrel.

A Sturdy and hard-hitting Assault Rifle. 
While on the heavier side of most ARs, it makes up for in it's sheer material penetration prowess.

Highly Modifed M4A1 Carbine, used primarily by US Special Forces under SOP Modifiactions. 
Nearly Identical to the AR-15, but sports calibur converstions and CQC Capability

Lesser known Bullpup around the world compared to the AUG. 
Unlike the AUG however, sports much more customizability without the use of a conversion.

Russia's Unique take on the AK Platform alongside the AK-12 and 15. 
It's first two bursts fire extremely fast and consistantly.
Has a rather unique reload well. Less Customization.

H&K designed AR, intended to replace the G3. 
Able to support a number of conversions, and sports familiar Iron-Sights.
Description

Standard Operations SMG for Covert Operations. 
It's Built-in Supressor decreases range.
A light, highly modular and extremely effective SMG indoors and in CQC Engagements.

Solid SMG chambered in .45 ACP. 
It's caliber makes it preform nearly on par with that of an AR.

Russian Developed SMG, based on the 74u. 
Rather Solid in terms of stats.

A rarer, 9mm version of the UMP design. 
While it does not have the firepower of it's sister, 45, it can be fired for a longer period of time thanks to it's 30rnd cap.

A compact, modular Sub-machine gun. 
It's design leaves it rather popular with Special Operations, allowing reloads to be a bit faster. Has only 20 rnds.

With a fire-rate higher than any SMG shown before, keeping ammo for this is going to be quite difficult. 
Good Damage output, but suffers from instability.
Like it's sibling, the Vector fires faster than any SMG before it, only this time, it uses a smaller, cheaper caliber.

A heavy, yet stable alternative to the popular MP5. 
The MPX supports modular attachment from the get-go, as a trade off for it's weight.
Weapon-system with the likeliness of a UMP, the LWRC.45 is a lighter, alternative to the UMP45, albiet more expensive.

A highly customizable AR platform with the ammunition conversion to that of a SMG. 
Excellent Modular Support, though not quite as loud caliber-wise.

Beligum's 50 Round Terror. 
With a caliber of 5.7x28, in a magazine of 50+1, the P90 makes up for it's lack of customizations with a hardhitting package.

Experimental PDW sporting a 6mm caliber designation. 
Standard across the board, it's simplicity makes it an easy weapon to pick up and use.

A smaller varient of the Uzi. 
Good for concealment and handles similar to the G18c, if not with it's slight differences.

Highly expiremental design, fusing the P90 cartridge to that of the AR-15. 
Less Customizable as a result. Has the properties of an SMG, but the handling of an AR.

column1: Sniper Rifles and Designated Marksmen Rifles

column6: Description

Semi-Automatic Sniper Rifle, in service to Russia's Armed Forces. 
Lacks the high range capabilities a normal sniper would have.
Taiwanese made Bolt Action Rifle. Holds 10 Shots. A generally good modular rifle.
Highly effective Bolt Action Rifle, capable of hitting long range distances so long as the shooter zeroes his target.

Standard Issue US Marksmen Rifle, known for it's long time in service. 
A reliable starter weapon for the aspiring sharpshooter.

Bolt Action Modular Sniper Rifle. 
Customizable design, with the ability to load AP rounds. Strong Sniper in General.

Designated Marksman Rifle developed by Knight's Armament Company. 
Modular and highly effective in any enviroment, but loses out to traditional bolt actions in penetration and range.

Highly Accurate Sniper Rifle made by Accuracy International. 
Extremely Popular and Highly Damaging, with Six Shots, Banned in several countries.

Compact Bolt-Action Sniper Rifle being developed by GmbH. 
Adopted by the German GSG9, and has shown remarkable marks for it's accuracy and ease of design.

Bolt Action Sniper Rifle developed by CheyTac inc. 
Uses easily accessible ammunition, rather than it's anti material ammunition counterparts.

Recovered Project from the Kremlin. 
Only a few were ever made. New Magazine and Design builds upon the Classic.
Semi-Automatic Bullpup Sniper, manufactured with recovered blueprints. It's design weighs it down slightly.

New Modular Sniper Rifle in development by the US Army to replace it's current Service Sniper. 
Takes notes off of the Remington MSR, but is overall designed for all-terrain fights.

An old proven design, modifed to its modern day counterparts. 
Now able to attach addons to it's gear. Pistol Grip and Stock puts it on par with it's competitors.

Highly Accurate .50 BMG Loaded Rifle from Britain. 
Highly Dangerous in capable hands. Requires Clearance.

Single-Shot, Bolt Action Austrian Anti Material Rifle. 
Not for the faint of heart. You'll need clearance before you think about fielding it.

column1: Light Machine Guns and Squad Support Weapons

column6: Description

Light Machine Gun varient of the AK Platform. 
The weight of an Assault Rifle with the power of an LMG.

LMG Variant of the G36. Sports a drum-mag and a rather consistant weapon design. 
Fairly customizable, and light to boot.

General Purpose Machine Gun chambered in 7.62. 
Still in service in a number of countries. 
Does not support modular support for most attachments.

An extremely heavy machine gun. 
While carrying it in general is rather difficult, the power behind it makes it a worth while support weapon. 150 rounds.

Lightweight LMG. 
Compared to the others, the M249 offers a lighter carry weight without sacrifcing fire power. 200 rounds.

Russian developed HMG. 
It's unique 150 Box Mag allows the user to sustain combat for longer, at the cost of weight.

American HMG in service to the US Army. 
Relatively light for it's class, it's stability is questionable at times.

Germany's refurbished Buzzsaw. 
Based on an old war design, the MG3 demonstrates solid stats as an LMG in it's class.
American Squad Support Weapon in service for the Marines. Handles relatively well and hits like a truck.
Description

Bullpup Shotgun. 
It's shells do not cover as much ground as the normal 12 gauge, but devastates single targets.

Pump Shotgun. 
Has a Semi Auto Mode, that allows it to fire off twice as much ammo as shooting it normally would.

Fully Automatic 12 Gauge. 
It's stability and accuracy is horrid, but the suppression and crowd control are excellent.

Highly Reliable Pump Action Shotgun. 
Can be customized for Short to Mid Range Engagements. 
A favorite amongst PMC Combat Units.

All-rounding shotgun sporting fantastic stability and shell displacement. 
Excellent for Short Range Engagements.

Don't fix something if it's not broken. 
Break-action Shotgun with a Two Shell Capacity. 
Has litterally no attachements, but why would you even?

A tube fed, semi-automatic shotgun. 
With 8 shots in the tube combined with it's ability to use slugs, the M1014 can be modified to suit any situation.

Mag-Fed 12 Gauge design, effective for sweeping buildings. 
It's effectiveness outside of CQC Enviroments leaves alot to be desired.

Widely used 12 Gauge Shotgun by Law Enforcement and PMCs alike. 
Simple yet extremely effective.

Unique Shotgun design sporting two barrels. 
Allows for two consecutive shots before the pump requires to be pulled back.

WIdely available civilian shotgun for home defense and hunting.
Starts with a 4 Shell Capacity that can be upgraded to 6.

Widely mass produced handgun. 
An upgraded varient from the 17, this time utilizing .45 ACP rounds.

Shironome's Service Pistol. 
One of the better sidearm choices available, although it lacks electronic sight support.

Russian Handgun chambered in 9 x 19mm. 
Large Magazine allows the user to fire off two shots rather than one.

A Modified M9 designed to fire in bursts. 
Without it's stock, the stability and accuracy of this weapon is rather bad.

Highly modular handgun on par with the USP. 
Features higher customizability as well as an Optic Mount.

New modular handgun being tested for official service use. 
Extremely Ergodynamic, and feels good to shoot.

Next Generation Special Operations handgun for usage in over several climate scenarios. 
It's rather bulky and heavy, but it comes with a Laser Module.

Beretta made Special Operations Handgun to work alongside the Beretta M9. 
A rather balanced HG at that. Can't go wrong with it. Barely fits any customization.

Old M1911A1 design spec dating back to 1964, auctioned off at a local munitions house. 
Surprisingly light-weight, and sports superior targeting capabilities.

Machine Pistol, capable of full auto fire. 
The weapon itself can be modified to become a PDW of sorts, though it'll take some practice before hand.

Belgium's 20 Round Modular Handgun. 
It's caliber is exceptional against Armored Opponents. 
Favorite Handgun for Spec-ops groups worldwide.

Exquisitely unique auto-loading magnum.
Created using Re-production Blueprints salvaged from Overseas Sorties
